front: Poem: meditation on death; lists "patriarchs," "kings," and "the powerful" joining an "innumerable caravan" — advises "go not like the quarry-slave at night"
Poem: mostly written at age 17; Richard Henry Dana thought it was a hoax, exclaiming "never written on this side of the water!"
Poem: asks a bird "dost thou pursue / Thy solitary way?" as its form is swallowed by "the abyss of heaven"
Author: longtime editor of the New York Evening Post; introduced Lincoln at the Cooper Union speech
Poem: contains "Truth, crushed to earth, shall rise again" — quoted by MLK in "Give Us the Ballot"
Author: called a "smooth, silent iceberg" in "supreme ice-olation" in Lowell's A Fable for Critics
